label: Netherlands / Dutch
ladderNote: Dutch youth/student relationship jargon from local Dutch sources (StudentenWoordenboek, Youngworks, DUB/Universiteit Utrecht, Instituut voor de Nederlandse Taal). Many labels are playful student slang, not universal adult Dutch.

levels.meaning: Nothing going on / single.
A click / chemistry. Generic Dutch phrasing before anything is defined.
Flirting / showing interest.
Dating or messaging, before a clear status.
One-off sex. Dutch dating glossaries use the English term and abbreviation.
Friends who also have sex, without a love relationship label.
Someone you regularly date or meet, but not a serious/exclusive relationship.
Twijfelscharrel: a doubtful scharrel where it is unclear which way things are going.
Kwaliteitsscharrel: a higher-quality scharrel with more feeling and potential.
Undefined romantic/sexual situation without a fixed relationship label.
Potential/eventual relationship; it could grow into a prela or rela.
Dating only each other, usually before calling it a relationship.
Pre-relatie: serious dating just before it becomes an official relationship.
Official relationship. Rela is the student/youth abbreviation; verkering is the older classic word.
Boyfriend/girlfriend or partner; established couple language.
Living apart together: a committed relationship while keeping separate homes.
Living together; a cohabitation contract can formalize shared arrangements.
Registered partnership; Dutch legal status close to marriage.
Engaged.
Married.
Married with kids.
